response.justification: Sarah has genuine strengths that map well to this role: current hands-on work in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, proven high-throughput distributed systems experience from Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP req/sec, and a multi-decade track record of staff-level technical breadth. However, Python is completely absent from the resume despite being a listed must-have, there is no mention of operational ownership or on-call responsibilities, and cross-functional stakeholder management is not evidenced. The AI-native work is current and credible, which tips the verdict toward a conditional yes rather than a hard pass.
strengths: Active contributor to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, directly matching the AI-native engineering baseline
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Staff-level title held at multiple companies with breadth across backend, frontend, embedded, and DevOps domains
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume — a listed must-have skill with no evidence of even working-level exposure
No mention of operational ownership, on-call rotation, or incident-response experience, which are explicit role requirements
Cross-functional stakeholder management and product-engineering bridging are not evidenced anywhere in the employment history
